causes: Damaged MAF sensor wiring or connector
fixes: Clean MAF sensor with MAF-specific cleaner
Inspect and repair wiring harness to MAF
Perform smoke test for vacuum leaks
Verify correct MAF sensor part number

causes: MAF sensor wiring short to ground
fixes: Check MAF sensor voltage output with scan tool
Inspect wiring for shorts or damage
Test MAF sensor signal voltage (should vary with RPM)
Check for wiring damage or chafing
Replace MAF sensor if out of spec
Dry out and seal connector if water damaged

causes: MAP sensor vacuum hose cracked or disconnected
fixes: Inspect MAP sensor vacuum hose and replace if cracked
Compare MAP sensor reading to barometric pressure at KOEO
Test MAP sensor with hand vacuum pump
Replace MAP sensor if readings erratic

causes: MAP sensor wiring short to ground
Open circuit in MAP signal wire
fixes: Check 5V reference voltage at MAP connector

causes: MAP sensor wiring short to power
fixes: Check MAP signal voltage (should be ~1V at idle)

causes: Open or short in signal circuit
fixes: Test IAT sensor resistance with multimeter
Check for water intrusion in connector

causes: IAT sensor reading extremely hot (short to ground)
Aftermarket intake too close to exhaust
IAT sensor reading extremely cold (open circuit)
fixes: Test IAT resistance (should be 2k-4k ohms at room temp)
Test ECT sensor resistance vs. temperature chart

causes: Thermostat stuck open causing slow warm-up
Faulty ECT sensor giving erratic readings
fixes: Monitor ECT sensor warm-up with scan tool
Replace ECT sensor if readings erratic

causes: Low coolant causing sensor to read air temp
fixes: Check coolant level and fill if low
Test ECT resistance (should not be infinite)
Check TPS voltage sweep with scan tool
Test TPS signal voltage at closed throttle (~0.5V)
Check TPS signal voltage at WOT (should be ~4.5V max)
Inspect wiring for shorts to power
Check throttle cable and pedal assembly

causes: Thermostat stuck open or opening too early
fixes: Replace thermostat with correct temperature rating
Verify coolant level and bleed air from system
Test ECT sensor against known temperature
Check fan relay and control module
Test O2 sensor signal with scan tool (should oscillate 0.1-0.9V)
Check for exhaust leaks with backpressure test
Perform smoke test for vacuum leaks
Replace O2 sensor if biased lean

causes: Short to power in O2 signal wire
fixes: Check for leaking injectors (fuel trim analysis)
Replace O2 sensor if shorted internally

causes: O2 sensor aged beyond useful life
fixes: Monitor O2 sensor switching speed with scan tool
Check for coolant or oil contamination in exhaust

causes: Wiring open circuit to O2 sensor
Blown fuse for O2 heater circuit
fixes: Check O2 heater fuse and relay
Test O2 sensor heater resistance (typically 4-40 ohms)
Inspect wiring from O2 sensor to ECM

causes: Wiring short or open in heater circuit
fixes: Test heater circuit resistance at sensor connector
Replace O2 sensor (heater not serviceable separately)
Repair wiring to O2 sensor heater

causes: Exhaust leak between catalyst and sensor
Catalytic converter failure affecting O2 reading
fixes: Check for exhaust leaks downstream of catalyst

causes: Wiring short to ground on O2 signal
fixes: Check downstream O2 signal with scan tool
Inspect wiring for shorts to ground

causes: Short to power in signal wire
fixes: Check for wiring short to voltage
Check O2 heater fuse and circuit
Verify catalytic converter is present and functional

causes: Wiring short or open in heater circuit
fixes: Test heater circuit voltage at connector
Test Bank 2 upstream O2 sensor signal

causes: Wiring fault in Bank 2 O2 heater circuit
fixes: Check fuse for Bank 2 O2 heater

causes: Wiring open or short in heater circuit

fixes: Check long-term and short-term fuel trims with scan tool
Smoke test intake for vacuum leaks

causes: Vacuum leak (intake manifold gasket, PCV, brake booster hose)
fixes: Smoke test intake system for leaks
Repair exhaust leaks before O2 sensor
Check fuel pressure regulator vacuum hose for fuel smell
Smoke test for vacuum leaks focusing on Bank 2
Inspect all intake hoses and clamps
Test purge valve with vacuum pump

causes: Clogged fuel filter causing pressure issues
fixes: Test fuel rail pressure sensor voltage

causes: Fuel rail pressure sensor out of range
fixes: Monitor fuel rail pressure with scan tool
Check fuel pressure at idle and under load
Replace fuel rail pressure sensor if erratic
Test injector pulse with noid light

causes: Open or short in cylinder 1 injector circuit
fixes: Test cylinder 1 injector with noid light
Check wiring from ECM to injector
Test cylinder 2 injector circuit with noid light
Check wiring from ECM to injector 2
Test noid light on cylinder 3 injector connector

causes: Open or short in injector wiring
fixes: Perform noid light test at cylinder 4
Replace injector if out of spec
Test cylinder 5 injector with noid light
Inspect water pump for impeller damage
Pressure test cooling system for leaks

causes: Internal transmission failure causing excessive heat
fixes: Check transmission fluid level and condition
Inspect transmission cooler lines and radiator cooler
Add auxiliary transmission cooler if towing

causes: Wiring fault in fuel pump circuit
fixes: Check for voltage at fuel pump connector
Replace fuel pump relay or pump as needed

causes: Fuel pump wiring short to ground
Wiring harness damage near fuel tank
fixes: Check fuel pump voltage at connector
Inspect wiring from relay to pump
Repair or replace fuel pump wiring
Replace fuel pump if drawing excessive current

causes: Short to power in fuel pump wiring
Faulty fuel pump relay stuck closed
fixes: Check fuel pump relay for stuck contacts
Inspect wiring for shorts to battery voltage
Test ECM fuel pump driver output

causes: Vacuum leak in boost control system
fixes: Test wastegate solenoid operation with scan tool
Inspect vacuum hoses to wastegate actuator
Verify wastegate opens and closes freely

causes: Wiring short to ground in wastegate circuit
Clogged air filter restricting turbo inlet
fixes: Inspect turbo compressor wheel for damage

causes: Cylinder 1 spark plug fouled or worn
Cylinder 1 fuel injector clogged or failed
Vacuum leak at cylinder 1 intake runner
fixes: Swap cylinder 1 coil to another cylinder to isolate fault
Check fuel injector pulse and flow
Replace spark plug and coil as needed

causes: Cylinder 2 spark plug worn or fouled
fixes: Swap coil from cylinder 2 to verify
Check injector operation with noid light
Perform intake valve cleaning if GDI engine

causes: Faulty spark plug or coil on cylinder 5
fixes: Swap coil and plug to isolate

causes: Crankshaft position system variation not learned
fixes: Perform CKP system variation learn procedure with scan tool
Drive vehicle through specific relearn conditions
Ensure no DTCs present before relearn
Consult service manual for exact procedure

causes: Engine detonation from low octane fuel
Loose or corroded knock sensor connector
Incorrect knock sensor torque during installation
fixes: Test knock sensor with scan tool (should respond to tapping near sensor)

causes: Wrong fuel octane causing persistent knock
fixes: Check knock sensor 2 wiring and connector
Test knock sensor signal with scan tool
Check CKP sensor output with oscilloscope
Inspect reluctor ring through sensor hole
Clean any metal debris from sensor
Monitor CKP signal with scan tool at various RPMs
Check wiring routing away from ignition wires
Check CMP sensor signal with scan tool or oscilloscope
Inspect CMP sensor wiring and connector

causes: CMP sensor signal out of range
fixes: Check camshaft timing with timing light or scan tool
Inspect CMP sensor mounting and air gap

causes: EGR vacuum hose disconnected or cracked
fixes: Replace EGR valve if stuck or damaged
Replace EGR valve if cleaning doesn't help

causes: EGR vacuum control stuck in open position
fixes: Check EGR valve for proper seating
Replace EGR valve if it won't close
Check AIR pump fuse and relay
Inspect check valve for exhaust backflow damage
Test AIR system with scan tool bi-directional control

causes: Exhaust leak between upstream and downstream O2
Oil or coolant contamination of catalyst
fixes: Monitor downstream O2 sensor voltage (should be steady ~0.5-0.7V)
Replace catalytic converter if efficiency below threshold
Fix any oil or coolant consumption issues

causes: Engine misfire on Bank 2 damaging catalyst
fixes: Fix any misfires before replacing catalyst
Verify upstream O2 sensors are functioning correctly
Check gas cap and tighten or replace

causes: Faulty purge valve (stuck open or closed)
fixes: Test purge valve with vacuum pump
Check purge valve resistance and operation
Inspect EVAP hoses for kinks or damage

causes: Faulty seal on purge or vent valve
fixes: Perform EVAP smoke test to find small leak
Inspect all EVAP hoses and connections
Clear code and monitor for return

causes: EVAP purge control valve circuit malfunction
Wiring short or open to purge valve
fixes: Test purge valve with bi-directional control
Repair wiring if open or shorted
Test vent valve operation with scan tool

causes: Fuel tank pressure sensor connector issue
fixes: Test fuel tank pressure sensor voltage
Check wiring for shorts to power
Ensure fuel tank not overfilled (stop at first click)

causes: Gas cap missing or very loose
Major EVAP hose disconnected or broken
Fuel tank puncture or seam leak
fixes: Inspect all EVAP hoses for disconnection
Check fuel filler neck for cracks
Replace gas cap with OEM part
Smoke test EVAP system for tiny leaks
Inspect and replace EVAP hoses if cracked
Clear code and drive through 2-3 complete EVAP monitors

causes: Wiring damage between sender and instrument cluster
fixes: Test fuel level sensor resistance at different fuel levels
Check wiring from fuel tank sender
Clean or replace fuel level sensor connector

causes: ABS wheel speed sensor fault affecting VSS signal
fixes: Test VSS signal with scan tool while driving

causes: Carbon buildup in idle air passage
fixes: Clean throttle body and IAC passage
Test IAC valve operation with scan tool
Clean throttle body and idle air passages
Replace PCV valve if stuck open

causes: Carbon buildup preventing throttle from seating
fixes: Check for vacuum leaks (especially intake manifold gasket)
Check throttle cable for proper free play
Replace IAC valve if stuck open
Test closed throttle position switch voltage

causes: Engine oil pressure sensor/switch circuit malfunction
Wiring fault to oil pressure sensor
fixes: Test oil pressure with mechanical gauge
Test oil pressure with mechanical gauge
Check oil pump and pickup tube if pressure low
Test battery condition and load test
Check for parasitic draw with ammeter
Replace battery or alternator as needed
Inspect drive belt condition and tension
Check battery cables and ground straps

causes: Aftermarket device interfering with CAN bus
fixes: Check CAN bus wiring with oscilloscope
Inspect ECM connectors for corrosion or damage
Check for aftermarket devices on CAN bus
Test ECM power and ground circuits
Replace ECM if internal failure confirmed
Attempt ECM reflash with latest calibration
Check battery voltage and charging system
Check for voltage spikes from bad alternator
Ensure stable battery voltage during programming (use battery tender)
Replace ECM if unresponsive to programming
Clear codes and drive to relearn fuel trims and idle
Check ECM power and ground circuits

causes: Voltage spike or low voltage condition
fixes: Check battery and charging system for voltage irregularities
Replace ECM if RAM error persists
Ensure clean power supply to ECM
Check all ECM power and ground circuits
Check for EMI sources near ECM wiring
Replace ECM if processor fault confirmed

causes: Wiring fault between TCM and ECM
fixes: Scan TCM for additional transmission codes
Check transmission fluid level and condition

causes: Wiring fault in brake switch circuit
Brake switch stuck on or off
fixes: Inspect wiring from brake switch to TCM/ECM

causes: Faulty TR sensor / neutral safety switch
fixes: Test TR sensor voltage at each gear position
Check shift linkage for worn bushings
Verify PRNDL display matches actual gear
Check transmission fluid level and condition
Test TFT sensor resistance vs. temperature
Inspect TFT sensor wiring and connector

causes: TFT sensor reading out of expected range
fixes: Monitor TFT sensor with scan tool during warm-up
Replace TFT sensor if readings erratic
Check wiring for shorts to ground
Check input speed sensor wiring and connector
Test input speed sensor signal with scan tool

causes: Damaged tone ring on output shaft
fixes: Test output speed sensor signal with scan tool
Check transmission fluid level and condition
Test for clutch slippage with stall test
Scan for specific gear ratio codes
Overhaul or replace transmission if mechanical failure
Check TCC solenoid operation with scan tool
Replace torque converter if clutch is worn
Monitor TCC slip speed with scan tool
Overhaul transmission if converter clutch worn

causes: Debris in valve body causing solenoid to stick
Wiring short causing solenoid to stay energized
fixes: Check transmission fluid condition for debris

causes: TCC solenoid wiring open or short
fixes: Check TCC solenoid wiring and connector
Monitor TCC operation over extended drive
Check TCC solenoid wiring for intermittent faults

causes: Pressure control solenoid A electrical fault
Wiring open or short to pressure solenoid
Shift solenoid A stuck or failed
Wiring fault to shift solenoid A
fixes: Check transmission fluid level and condition
Check shift solenoid A operation with scan tool

causes: Shift solenoid B stuck or failed
Wiring fault to shift solenoid B
fixes: Inspect solenoid B wiring and connector
Check solenoid B operation with scan tool

causes: Solenoid B wiring open or shorted
Shift solenoid C stuck or failed
fixes: Check solenoid C wiring and connector

causes: Shift solenoid D stuck or failed
fixes: Check solenoid D wiring and connector

causes: Shift solenoid E stuck or failed
fixes: Check solenoid E wiring and connector
Check transmission fluid level and condition
Rebuild or replace transmission if multiple solenoids fail

causes: Rich fuel mixture degrading NOx trap
fixes: Check for engine misfires or rich running conditions
Use quality fuel with low sulfur content
Check for exhaust leaks downstream of catalyst
Replace downstream O2 sensor if biased

causes: Oil or coolant burning in exhaust
fixes: Monitor downstream O2 sensor for stuck rich
Check for oil or coolant consumption
Replace downstream O2 sensor if biased

causes: Electronic throttle control motor circuit range/performance
Throttle control system forced idle (limp mode)
TCM or ECM fault triggering limp mode
fixes: Check for related throttle codes (P2101, P2111, P2112)
Replace throttle body if motor faulty

causes: Throttle actuator control system forced limited power
fixes: Diagnose and repair primary throttle code first
Replace throttle body if actuator motor failed

causes: Throttle actuator control system forced limited RPM
Severe engine or transmission issue triggering protection
fixes: Check throttle plate movement manually (key off)
Replace throttle body assembly if needed

causes: Carbon buildup holding throttle plate open
Throttle body motor failure (stuck open position)
fixes: Replace throttle body if plate won't close

causes: Carbon buildup holding throttle plate closed
fixes: Check throttle plate for free movement
Replace throttle body if plate stuck

causes: Throttle plate position not matching commanded
fixes: Check for TSB on throttle body
Replace throttle body if relearn fails
Monitor throttle position with scan tool

causes: TPS sensor A/B voltage correlation error
Wiring issue between TPS and ECM
fixes: Check TPS A and B voltage signals (should be 2:1 ratio)

causes: APP sensor D/E voltage correlation error
fixes: Check APP sensor D and E signal voltages
Inspect APP sensor wiring and connector

causes: O2 sensor signal stuck lean (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
fixes: Monitor upstream O2 signal with scan tool
Replace O2 sensor if biased lean

causes: O2 sensor signal stuck rich (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
fixes: Replace O2 sensor if biased rich

causes: O2 sensor signal stuck lean (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
O2 sensor signal stuck rich (Bank 1 Sensor 2)

fixes: Inspect all intake hoses and connections
Check brake booster for vacuum leaks

causes: EVAP system leak detection pump stuck on
Wiring short to ground in charge indicator circuit
Wiring short to power in charge circuit
ECM/PCM internal engine off timer performance
fixes: Check for voltage interruption to ECM
Replace ECM if internal clock faulty
Test O2/AF sensor response with scan tool
Check for silicone or coolant contamination
Monitor downstream O2 sensor with scan tool

causes: Oil pressure too low for VVT/deactivation
Low oil level or wrong viscosity
fixes: Monitor oil pressure with scan tool

causes: Cylinder deactivation solenoid Bank 1 circuit fault
Wiring open or short to solenoid
